bibliographic_data.title: Package for Fast ABC-Boost
bibliographic_data.abstract: This report presents the open-source package https://github.com/pltrees/abcboost which implements a series of boosting works over the past many years (Li, 2008, 2009, 2010a,b; Li and Zhao, 2022a,c). In particular, this package includes mainly three lines of techniques, among which two are already standard implementations in popular boosted tree platforms: (i) The histogram-based approach makes the tree implementation convenient and efficient. (ii) The explicit gain formula for tree splitting based on second-order derivatives of the loss function typically improves over first-order methods. The main contribution is the ABC-Boost (adaptive base class boosting) for multi-class classification, which can substantially improve classification accuracy if the base class is chosen properly.

core_content.novelty_claim: The package introduces a new framework called 'Fast ABC-Boost' with parameters to flexibly and efficiently choose the proper search space for the base class in multi-class classification tasks.
core_content.key_methodology: The package implements techniques such as histogram-based feature binning, explicit gain formula based on second-order derivatives of the loss function, and adaptive base class boosting (ABC-Boost) for multi-class classification. The ABC-Boost method rewrites the derivatives of the classical multi-class logistic regression loss function to improve accuracy.
outcomes: Improved accuracy in multi-class classification tasks through proper selection of the base class
Flexibility and efficiency in choosing the search space for the base class
core_content.full_text_markdown: The 'Fast ABC-Boost' package is an open-source implementation that combines a series of boosting techniques developed over several years. It includes methods such as histogram-based feature binning, explicit gain formula based on second-order derivatives, and adaptive base class boosting (ABC-Boost) for multi-class classification. The package aims to improve the accuracy of multi-class classification tasks by re-writing the derivatives of the classical multi-class logistic regression loss function. This approach can substantially enhance performance if the base class is chosen properly. The main contribution is a unified framework called 'Fast ABC-Boost' that introduces parameters to flexibly and efficiently choose the search space for the base class.

nodes.label: Explicit Gain Formula for Tree Splitting
